Package org.rodinp.core.tests

Class TestInternalManipulation

    Ensures that the next sibling of an internal element is computed appropriately, when there is one other sibling.

    • testNextSiblingInexistent

      public void testNextSiblingInexistent() throws Exception
      Ensures that trying to access to the next sibling of an inexistent element throws the appropriate exception.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testNextSibling_1

      public void testNextSibling_1() throws Exception
      Ensures that the next sibling of an internal element is computed appropriately, when there is no other sibling.
      Throws:
      Exception

      public void testNextSibling_2() throws Exception
      Ensures that the next sibling of an internal element is computed appropriately, when there is one other sibling.
      Throws:
      Exception

      public void testNextSibling_3() throws Exception
      Ensures that the next sibling of an internal element is computed appropriately, when there are two other siblings.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testCreateNewChildNonRootInvalidType

      public void testCreateNewChildNonRootInvalidType() throws RodinDBException
      Ensures that an element which type is invalid for a child of a non-root parent element cannot be created.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • testAddUbiquitousChild

      public void testAddUbiquitousChild() throws Exception
      Ensures that no exception is thrown when creating a ubiquitous child.
      Throws:
      Exception

      Throws:

      public void testAPIGettersForUbiquitous() throws Exception
      Ensures that element type API methods return expected values when handling ubiquitous items.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testAPIGettersForExplicitUbiquitousRelationship

      public void testAPIGettersForExplicitUbiquitousRelationship() throws Exception
      An explicit relationship extension is declared from NamedElement2 parent to ubiquitous child element and attribute. Verify that the API also makes it explicit.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testAddExplicitUbiquitous

      public void testAddExplicitUbiquitous() throws Exception
      An explicit relationship extension is declared from NamedElement2 parent to ubiquitous child element and attribute. Verify that we can indeed create child and attribute.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testAddChildToUbiquitous

      public void testAddChildToUbiquitous() throws Exception
      Ensures that no exception is thrown when creating child of an ubiquitous element (i.e. also checks that an ubiquitous element can parent a named element).
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testAddAttributeToUbiquitous

      public void testAddAttributeToUbiquitous() throws Exception
      Ensures that no exception is thrown when creating child of an ubiquitous element (i.e. also checks that an ubiquitous element can parent a named element).
      Throws:
      Exception
